note: T0.1 artifact, updated per the T0.2 fit decision (docs/T0.2-taxonomy-examples.md). Canonical record of anchor regulations and codebase roles.

regulations.doc: RBI (Commercial Banks - Credit Cards and Debit Cards: Issuance and Conduct) Directions, 2025
regulations.archived_pin: data/regulations/sources/MANIFEST.json (doc_role cc_2025_current_anchor; predecessor 2022 PDFs pinned there too)
regulations.repeals: RBI Master Direction - Credit Card and Debit Card - Issuance and Conduct Directions, 2022 (issued 2022-04-21, amended 2024-03-07), repealed for commercial banks 2025-11-28 vide DOR.RRC.REC.302/33-01-010/2025-26

predecessor.doc: RBI Master Direction - Credit Card and Debit Card - Issuance and Conduct Directions, 2022
predecessor.note: Repealed & reissued as this 2025 instrument. Substantive values carried over UNCHANGED; clauses renumbered into paragraphs 1-97 across 8 chapters. Supplies T6 old sides (2022 vs 2024 vs 2025 versions) and the 2022->2025 supersession pair.

regulations.notes: RE-ANCHORED 2026-07-09 after T2.1 re-verification: the 2022 Master Direction was repealed for commercial banks on 2025-11-28 and reissued as this entity-specific instrument (part of RBI's 2025-11-28 restructuring wave that also produced the KYC Directions 2025). CardDemo is a commercial-bank card-servicing system, so this is the correct in-force anchor. KYC/AML compliance is incorporated by reference at 2025 paragraph 90 (was clause 29 in the repealed 2022 MD), making the union with the KYC Directions legally coherent. Clause records in data/regulations/clauses.jsonl are anchored here with 2025 paragraph numbers secondary-mapped from a text reproduction; the 2025 primary PDF pass to confirm numbering is a T2.5 obligation.

regulations.archived_pin: data/regulations/sources/MANIFEST.json (doc_role kyc_2025; 2016 consolidation + 2023/2024 amendment circulars pinned there too)
regulations.notes: Anchor for the real-curated seed (T2.5) and the T6 versioned-judgment pairs (2016 vs 2025 clause versions). Reached from the primary clause set via the CC Directions KYC/AML bridge at 2025 paragraph 90 (was clause 29 in the repealed 2022 MD). Justification: rich rule logic for D1/D2/D4/D6, and the 2016-to-2025 replacement is genuine real-world drift, not a construction. Pure-KYC logic with no CardDemo host is implemented in the GnuCOBOL-native runnable base. KYC clause_ids in clauses.jsonl remain PROVISIONAL pending the primary read of the 2025 Directions at T2.5.

Only if used: pin exact component + version + effective date (e.g. Credit Risk SA Directions 2026, effective 2027-04-01; LCR guidelines effective 2026-04-01). Basel is not one document; do not cite it unpinned.

codebases.name: AWS CardDemo
codebases.notes: All T0.2 taxonomy loci and line numbers refer to this commit. Cleanest license for public benchmark release; credit-card domain dense with regulated thresholds; batch CB* programs are the GnuCOBOL-runnable subset. Justification: synthetic data, built to be analyzed, and the T0.2 pass found naturally-occurring drift-shaped code (missing ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS check) confirming domain fit.

codebases.name: IBM CICS Bank Sample Application (CBSA)
codebases.notes: Account/loan domain complementing CardDemo's card servicing. CICS-heavy; verification on this corpus will skew to Tiers 2-3. Pin commit when first used.

codebases.name: GnuCOBOL-native banking sample (small, MIT)
codebases.notes: Host for run_cobol harness fixtures, synthetic conformant programs, and pure-KYC logic that has no CardDemo host (per the T0.2 fit decision). Selection and pinning due at T1.5/T2.5 start.
